# CoinEx.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the CoinEx REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports CoinEx Spot V2, margin account operations, Futures, account/asset operations, typed market data, order placement, WebSocket subscriptions, local order books, and shared multi-exchange abstractions through CryptoExchange.Net.

CoinEx.Net is the C# client for CoinEx in the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem. REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` / `HttpResult`, WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`, and shared symbol/cache helpers return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`. It also provides typed REST and WebSocket clients, automatic WebSocket reconnection, client-side rate limiting, native AOT support, and shared API interfaces for exchange-agnostic applications. Current version: 10.10.0. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, usage examples, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/CoinEx.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules
- [Claude/Codex Skill](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/CLAUDE.md): Compact generation rules for assistants

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/tree/master/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balance, limit order placement, order status check
- [Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): Futures leverage, market order, position retrieval, position close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker subscription, order book/trade subscriptions, authenticated user streams with proper teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis, capability discovery, and shared subscriptions
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): HttpResult, WebSocketResult, and ExchangeCallResult patterns
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/tree/master/Examples): Console, API, order book, and tracker examples

## Critical Usage Rules

- Use `CoinExRestClient` for REST and `CoinExSocketClient` for WebSocket.
- Use `CoinExCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, when configuring API keys.
- Use `SpotApiV2` for current spot V2 REST examples.
- Use `FuturesApi` for CoinEx futures.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- For spot trading methods, pass `AccountType.Spot` or `AccountType.Margin`.
- For futures leverage, use `FuturesApi.Account.SetLeverageAsync(symbol, MarginMode.Cross/Isolated, leverage)`.
- Do not copy Binance-only concepts such as USD-M/COIN-M branches, `timeInForce`, `SpotOrderType`, `FuturesOrderType`, or Binance testnet environments.

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by CoinEx.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/CoinEx.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet

- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/MSpeEtSY8t): Maintainer-supported Discord for questions and discussion
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/CoinEx.Net/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
- [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/JKorf): Support the maintainer
